primary_story_title: Delegate visual demand and consume evaluated geometry
requirement: The Pipeline shall consume accepted VAE Asset Results only after validating request/result identity, source and derivative lineage, asset-family scope, measured geometry, masks, gaze or motion, depth/layers, validated crops, negative-space regions, limitations, evaluation evidence, and downstream compatibility.
required_evidence: Demand/result match report, geometry validation, evaluator receipts, artifact hashes, and acknowledgement/usage receipt.

title: Keep SAM3 execution inside the Visual Asset Editor
primary_story_title: Preserve VAE ownership of SAM3, Lucida, layered providers, and GNM
requirement: SAM3 segmentation and tracking shall be implemented, versioned, evaluated, and operated by the Visual Asset Editor behind its provider and capability interfaces; the Pipeline owns only demand, result, health, and receipt integration.
required_evidence: Product-boundary test, VAE capability receipt, Delegation result, and Pipeline consumer conformance.

title: Keep Lucida matting evaluation inside the Visual Asset Editor
primary_story_title: Preserve VAE ownership of SAM3, Lucida, layered providers, and GNM
requirement: Lucida or any replacement matting provider may be evaluated and, if authorized, executed only within the VAE capability registry and production plan; the Pipeline shall not own provider selection or matting semantics.
required_evidence: Capability registry state, benchmark report, dependency adoption review, output hashes, and result receipt.

title: Keep layered visual generation and GNM behind VAE capability routing
primary_story_title: Preserve VAE ownership of SAM3, Lucida, layered providers, and GNM
requirement: Layered image providers, ComfyUI workflows, and Google GNM facial-geometry references shall remain VAE-owned capabilities selected by the Visual Production Plan; they are not Pipeline, Interview Expression, or identity-authority components.
required_evidence: VAE route receipt, source-reference binding, GNM/version identity, geometry outputs, fairness/limitation acknowledgement, and independent evaluation.

title: Validate Asset Results and downstream usage before continuation
primary_story_title: Delegate visual demand and consume evaluated geometry
requirement: The Pipeline shall resume composition only after the VAE result passes demand identity, authority, source lineage, geometry, evaluation, artifact integrity, supersession, and usage-acknowledgement checks.
required_evidence: Validation report, usage acknowledgement, asset/version lineage, and selective invalidation receipt.

title: Verify mechanically decidable conditions
primary_story_title: Run deterministic and independent evaluation
requirement: Contracts, geometry, text fit, timing, source lineage, tool use, export and execution-envelope conditions shall use deterministic verification.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Evaluate judgment dimensions independently
primary_story_title: Run deterministic and independent evaluation
requirement: Separate evaluators shall assess semantic fidelity, Activative function, category-native grammar, composition, temporal effect and wrong-reading risk.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Reparse rendered Visual Syntax
primary_story_title: Reparse rendered syntax and diagnose the responsible layer
requirement: Static and temporal outputs shall be parsed back into observed BBOX, hierarchy, reading-order and timing evidence for comparison with the intended contract.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Attribute failures to responsible layers
primary_story_title: Reparse rendered syntax and diagnose the responsible layer
requirement: Diagnosis shall distinguish knowledge, retrieval, context, Programmed Model, tool, runtime, VAE and evaluator defects.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Repair only declared units
primary_story_title: Repair only declared units and escalate human-owned conflicts
requirement: A repair shall change only the diagnosed unit and its permitted dependent units while preserving already-valid relationships.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Escalate human-owned conflicts
primary_story_title: Repair only declared units and escalate human-owned conflicts
requirement: Unresolved taste, new meaning, identity/operator source authorization and policy exceptions shall route to attributable human authority.
required_evidence: deterministic validators, evaluator calibration, rendered reparse, diagnosis accuracy, selective rerun, and escalation, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Persist complete production and human-resolution episodes
primary_story_title: Persist complete production episodes and causal attribution
requirement: Each accepted, rejected, repaired, escalated, and human-resolved production episode shall link the source package, Harness, run state, JIT Capsule, candidates, exact tool operations, renders, evaluations, decision authority, result, and downstream outcome.
required_evidence: Episode hash, before/after references, tool/process receipts, human authority, and result lineage.

title: Attribute learning opportunities to the responsible layer
primary_story_title: Persist complete production episodes and causal attribution
requirement: The system shall diagnose whether an episode indicates a source, knowledge, retrieval, context, Skill, Steering Recipe, Programmed Model, tool, runtime, VAE, evaluator, or human-policy opportunity before it enters any learning queue.
required_evidence: Diagnosis report, responsible-layer confidence, excluded alternatives, and human review where required.

title: Compile operator-authorized versioned programming-material corpora
primary_story_title: Build operator-authorized datasets and promote recipes safely
requirement: Supervised demonstrations, preference pairs, hard negatives, repair trajectories, retrieval-obedience cases, and revision-program examples shall be built offline from eligible episodes with exact source authority, operator source authorization, provenance, scope, splits, hashes, and invalidation.
required_evidence: Dataset manifest, operator-declared source authority report, split hashes, deduplication, leakage tests, and build receipt.

title: Capture human preferences and corrections with explicit scope
primary_story_title: Build operator-authorized datasets and promote recipes safely
requirement: Every operator approval, rejection, candidate selection, natural-language revision, direct manipulation, tool override, taste explanation, and publication decision shall produce a HumanResolutionEpisode whose applicability scope is explicit and nonuniversal by default.
required_evidence: Episode record, authority scope, exact changes, tool program, evaluation, and acceptance.

title: Promote Steering Recipes only through repeated evidence
primary_story_title: Build operator-authorized datasets and promote recipes safely
requirement: Repeated successful, scoped interventions may advance a Steering Recipe through proposed, experimental, validated, shadow, limited-production, production, deprecated, and retired states with control comparisons, regression cases, human authority, and rollback.
required_evidence: Recipe evidence ledger, control comparison, regression suite, human promotion receipt, and rollback package.

title: Prohibit uncontrolled online mutation while enabling automatic evidence capture
primary_story_title: Build operator-authorized datasets and promote recipes safely
requirement: Production may automatically append immutable episodes, retrieval indexes, and programming-material eligibility records, but it shall not rewrite model weights, canonical Skills, Steering Recipes, authority rules, or production defaults without an offline evaluated release and human authorization.
required_evidence: Mutation-denial tests, index update receipt, model artifact immutability checks, and promotion workflow.

title: Emit composite execution-stack fingerprints
primary_story_title: Fingerprint and sandbox every node
requirement: Every node attempt shall record the authorized and resolved model, context, tools, retrieval indexes, runtime, precision, hardware and evaluator identities.
required_evidence: fingerprints, sandbox denials, process receipts, canary/drift metrics, containment and restore tests, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Execute inside declared sandboxes
primary_story_title: Fingerprint and sandbox every node
requirement: Nodes shall have explicit file, network, tool, cache, time, compute and side-effect permissions.
required_evidence: fingerprints, sandbox denials, process receipts, canary/drift metrics, containment and restore tests, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Record observable process receipts
primary_story_title: Fingerprint and sandbox every node
requirement: Tool calls, resource access, state mutations, validation calls, denied actions and external requests shall be independently logged.
required_evidence: fingerprints, sandbox denials, process receipts, canary/drift metrics, containment and restore tests, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Revalidate material changes
primary_story_title: Continuously assure and contain execution regressions
requirement: Changes to models, prompts, retrieval, tools, quantization, preprocessing, evaluator or runtime semantics shall trigger focused regression.
required_evidence: fingerprints, sandbox denials, process receipts, canary/drift metrics, containment and restore tests, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Monitor production signals
primary_story_title: Continuously assure and contain execution regressions
requirement: The system shall track hard-gate failures, acceptance, candidates per result, repairs, escalations, retrieval errors, cost, latency and evaluator disagreement.
required_evidence: fingerprints, sandbox denials, process receipts, canary/drift metrics, containment and restore tests, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Contain and restore incidents
primary_story_title: Continuously assure and contain execution regressions
requirement: Affected claims may be quarantined, routed to a known-good fallback and restored only after regression evidence and release approval.
required_evidence: fingerprints, sandbox denials, process receipts, canary/drift metrics, containment and restore tests, linked to an immutable requirement receipt.

title: Expose source-to-batch run and graph projections
primary_story_title: Operate the source-to-batch Control Tower
requirement: The Studio Control Tower shall show the Canonical Interview Source Package, content-batch jobs, Harnesses, phases, Workflow Nodes, bounded roles, owners, status, blockers, costs, artifacts, source-time lineage, and exact execution evidence.
required_evidence: Read-model consistency tests, realtime event mapping, source-lineage drill-down, and projection freshness receipts.

title: Provide category-native supervisory previews over canonical programs
primary_story_title: Operate the source-to-batch Control Tower
requirement: Static, video, interview, VAE, and knowledge/model Studio modules shall display the current canonical programs, source evidence, assets, candidates, evaluations, repairs, and allowed human actions without owning hidden production state.
required_evidence: Round-trip tests, command receipts, reload/replay tests, and UI/canonical-state consistency.

title: Apply Harness autonomy modes and explicit human gates
primary_story_title: Launch campaigns and govern autonomy and exceptions
requirement: Each campaign shall operate in AUTOPILOT, REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP, CHECKPOINTED, or SHADOW mode, pausing only at declared human authority nodes, genuine exceptions, budget or policy changes, or unresolved taste boundaries.
required_evidence: Autonomy binding, pause/resume logs, authority tests, and publication-denial receipt.

title: Inspect retrieval, Skills, recipes, Programmed Models, and exclusions
primary_story_title: Operate the source-to-batch Control Tower
requirement: Operators shall be able to inspect the JIT Capsule, retrieved Skills and Steering Recipes, authority filters, exclusions, contradictory evidence, model claim envelope, benchmark evidence, limitations, fallbacks, and the reason each item influenced the current decision.
required_evidence: Retrieval receipt, claim manifest, benchmark links, and UI evidence tests.

title: Expose runtime, provider, worker, and budget health
primary_story_title: Operate the source-to-batch Control Tower
requirement: The Studio shall expose preflight, compatibility, health, queue, lease, resource, cost, and failure evidence for Skia, PRETEXT, FFmpeg, Remotion, HyperFrames, VAE providers, GNM, workers, and other bound embodiments without treating their presence as production certification.
required_evidence: Health receipts, worker heartbeats, budget ledger, blocker records, and security tests.

title: Export source-to-ship audit and replay packages
primary_story_title: Operate the source-to-batch Control Tower
requirement: A completed campaign shall export the source package manifest, content-batch plan, Harnesses and bindings, JIT Capsules, typed programs, HumanResolutionEpisodes, artifacts, evaluations, execution fingerprints, logs, publication evidence, and replay instructions needed to reconstruct the accepted result.
required_evidence: Portable bundle validation, hash manifest, secret/path scan, and clean-room replay report.

title: Build the source-first interview-to-content-batch reference slice
primary_story_title: Prove the real source-first Release 1 slice
requirement: Release 1 shall admit one real Activative Interview or imported interview, produce an accepted Canonical Interview Source Package, launch a source-backed batch, render one talking-head A-roll short and one Carousel or SuperVisual from the same source, execute bounded evaluation and repair, record at least one HumanResolutionEpisode, and preserve exact source lineage.
required_evidence: Real-file hashes, source-time maps, render/evaluation receipts, HumanResolutionEpisode, and operator acceptance.

title: Require complete source-first release evidence
primary_story_title: Prove the real source-first Release 1 slice
requirement: The Release 1 slice shall prove source authority and identity, dual-admission correctness, Harness and binding authority, retrieval relevance, program validity, renderer correctness, independent evaluation, local repair, human resolution capture, sandbox, cancellation/replay, fallback, and truthful claim ceilings.
required_evidence: Case-family reports, evaluator metrics, recovery/replay, source authority/operator source authorization evidence, and release receipt.

title: Reconcile current repository and operator-local truth before implementation
primary_story_title: Reconcile authority and predecessor state
requirement: Implementation shall not begin until public and operator-local Builder, VAE, Delegation, Studio predecessor, campaign-amendment, and source-crosswalk states are reconciled into one Program Control status without rewriting historical receipts.
required_evidence: Status reconciliation report, source manifests, conflict dispositions, and Program Control receipt.

title: Expand one source, category, runtime, provider, or learned claim boundary at a time
primary_story_title: Expand boundaries and maintain truthful claim states
requirement: Every expansion beyond Release 1 shall declare its source-package requirements, category/profile, derivative contract, runtime or provider binding, learned claim envelope, evaluator profile, source authority scope, rollback, and scope-specific evidence.
required_evidence: Expansion manifest, benchmark/evaluation plan, compatibility tests, and authorization receipt.

title: Separate implementation, evidence closure, production readiness, and certification
primary_story_title: Expand boundaries and maintain truthful claim states
requirement: Feature code, local tests, successful real outputs, production readiness, evaluator certification, and product certification shall remain separate claim states with independent evidence and authority.
required_evidence: Claim-state tests, evidence matrix, status receipts, and UI labeling checks.

title: Generate a bounded PRD-to-implementation handoff
primary_story_title: Generate the canonical implementation handoff
requirement: After human ratification, the PRD shall produce a canonical FR ledger, value-based Epics, vertical CBAR-hardened Stories, Tech Spec candidates, exact source reuse dispositions, target paths, tests, evidence gates, Development Capsules, and authority boundaries without treating the planning package as implementation authorization.
required_evidence: Traceability validation, Story/FR ownership report, source-crosswalk validation, and planning receipt.

title: Establish the Canonical Interview Source Package as the batch root
primary_story_title: Admit an Activative Interview session
requirement: The product shall represent each interview-derived campaign with one immutable Canonical Interview Source Package that binds the accepted source media, transcript, speakers, timestamps, tags, Expression Moments, keyframes, source authority, and provenance used by every derivative job.
required_evidence: Package schema validation, source/hash manifest, lineage graph, and acceptance receipt.

title: Support Activative Interview Brief-led admission
primary_story_title: Admit an Activative Interview session
requirement: The Interview Expression integration shall admit sessions produced from an Activative Interview Brief, preserving planned Context Premises, Matrix of Edging, anchors, Interview Asset Contracts, intended tags, operator source authorization, and session design separately from what the participant actually expressed.
required_evidence: Brief hash, session operator source authorization, planned/observed tag comparison, and Interview Expression acceptance receipt.

title: Support imported interview and transcript admission as a first-class path
primary_story_title: Admit an imported interview and align it
requirement: The product shall admit an existing interview video with an uploaded or generated transcript without pretending that an Activative Interview Brief, Matrix of Edging, planned anchors, or predeclared asset contracts existed.
required_evidence: Import receipt, source/transcript hashes, absent-field declaration, retrospective-tag evidence, and operator confirmation.

title: Align transcript, speakers, time bases, and media technically
primary_story_title: Admit an imported interview and align it
requirement: The source-package compiler shall reconcile word-level timestamps, phrase boundaries, speaker diarization, audio events, source frame/time bases, media duration, and transcript confidence before source spans become eligible.
required_evidence: Alignment benchmark, duration/time-base validation, diarization review samples, and phrase-pack hash.

title: Govern operator-declared source authority, operator-defined source handling, and derivative usage
primary_story_title: Admit an Activative Interview session
requirement: The source package shall declare participant operator source authorization, ownership, allowed derivative formats, publication scope, model-training eligibility, identity/voice use, retention, source revocation, and restricted evidence before any job or learning disposition is admitted.
required_evidence: Operator source-authority declaration, provenance checks, scope tests, usage audit, and source-revocation simulation.

title: Version, supersede, and selectively invalidate source packages
primary_story_title: Version, supersede, and invalidate source packages
requirement: Corrections to transcripts, speakers, tags, source authority, Expression Moments, or source assets shall create a new immutable source-package version and compute selective downstream invalidation rather than rewriting accepted history.
required_evidence: Version/hash comparison, dependency analysis, selective invalidation tests, and historical replay.

title: Separate planned, observed, inferred, confirmed, rejected, and superseded tags
primary_story_title: Separate tag provenance and compile phrase context
requirement: Every source and routing tag shall declare its provenance state, evidence, confidence, authority, applicability, and lifecycle so an intended interview topic cannot be confused with an observed Expression Moment or an operator-confirmed route.
required_evidence: Tag-state registry tests, provenance graph, evidence links, and exclusion receipt.

title: Use the packed phrase-level transcript as the primary language reasoning surface
primary_story_title: Separate tag provenance and compile phrase context
requirement: The Pipeline shall compile a compact, speaker-aware, time-annotated phrase transcript from cached word-level data and use it as the default text reasoning surface, loading raw words or visual composites only when the current decision requires them.
required_evidence: Phrase-pack hash, compression coverage, source-word backreferences, and retrieval/context-size benchmark.

title: Build source shot maps, transitions, keyframes, and visual references
primary_story_title: Build shot maps and keyframe references
requirement: The source-package compiler shall detect existing shot boundaries and transitions, sample representative and expression-relevant keyframes, record camera/framing changes, and expose visual references without treating shot changes as semantic importance.
required_evidence: Detection benchmark, frame hashes, source-time validation, and sampled human review.

title: Discover and approve Anchor Hits and Expression Moments
primary_story_title: Discover and approve Expression Moments
requirement: A bounded Expression Moment Hunter may propose source-backed spans using transcript, audio events, keyframes, Brief context, tags, and evidence; an Analyst validates truth and completeness, and only the authorized approval path may promote an Expression Moment.
required_evidence: Candidate portfolio, source-context review, Analyst report, approval receipt, and false-positive/false-negative benchmark.

title: Preserve rejected and borderline expression evidence
primary_story_title: Discover and approve Expression Moments
requirement: Rejected, incomplete, invalid, low-confidence, or borderline Anchor Hit and Expression Moment candidates shall be stored with reasons, responsible failure layer, and future admissibility conditions rather than discarded.
required_evidence: Lifecycle and retrieval tests, rejection reasons, source authority scope, and supersession history.

title: Compile an Expression Ingredient Inventory and Asset Package Spec
primary_story_title: Compile the Expression Ingredient Inventory and Asset Package Spec
requirement: Interview Expression shall compile approved quotes, evidence, Expression Moments, keyframes, voice/audio references, visual references, tags, archetype opportunities, restrictions, and lineage into an Asset Package Spec that downstream Atomic Harnesses can consume without rediscovering the source.
required_evidence: Schema validation, completeness report, ingredient/source graph, restrictions, and handoff receipt.

title: Compile one source-backed content-batch program
primary_story_title: Compile the content batch and derivative jobs
requirement: The Pipeline shall compile a Content Batch Orchestration Program from one accepted source package or Asset Package Spec, declaring target derivative jobs, Harnesses, shared ingredients, dependencies, budgets, deadlines, review modes, and source-lineage obligations.
required_evidence: Batch schema, dependency graph, budget plan, Harness binding validation, and compilation receipt.

title: Route approved expression material through eligible archetypes and Harnesses
primary_story_title: Route archetypes, reuse analysis, and prevent duplicate outputs
requirement: The Pipeline shall route approved expression material through current archetype coalitions, Primitive Coalition Contracts, asset-derivative routes, category/profile registries, and Builder-produced Harnesses; archetypes are psychological geometries that organize the viewer role inside tension, not decorative labels.
required_evidence: Eligibility tests, route ranking benchmark, exclusion reasons, and route-selection receipt.

title: Create immutable derivative job contracts
primary_story_title: Compile the content batch and derivative jobs
requirement: Each batch job shall bind one Harness version, exact source-package and ingredient references, derivative type, target platform/profile, intended Activative and sequence function, output constraints, runtime capabilities, evaluation, source authority, and review policy.
required_evidence: Job schema, authority/source authority filters, source-grant validation, and admission receipt.

title: Share source analysis while preventing duplicate or conflicting derivatives
primary_story_title: Route archetypes, reuse analysis, and prevent duplicate outputs
requirement: The batch runtime shall reuse accepted source transcripts, phrase packs, shot maps, keyframes, and approved moments, deduplicate overlapping derivative candidates, and detect conflicting source uses or near-duplicate outputs before production spend.
required_evidence: Reuse-cache tests, overlap/dedupe report, diversity evidence, and cost savings receipt.

title: Represent Hunters, Analysts, Composers, and Commanders as bounded workflow roles
primary_story_title: Execute explicit nodes and bounded production roles
requirement: The Pipeline shall model Hunter, Analyst, Composer, and Commander responsibilities as explicit node roles with separate capabilities, tools, inputs, outputs, authority, and failure handling rather than hidden personas or a monolithic multi-agent prompt.
required_evidence: Role/actor taxonomy tests, handoff contracts, authority matrix, and node receipts.

title: Propagate source or batch changes through selective invalidation
primary_story_title: Propagate upstream change selectively
requirement: When a source package, approved moment, route, source authority state, Harness, or shared ingredient is superseded, the batch runtime shall compute and rerun only affected jobs and descendants while preserving accepted unaffected work.
required_evidence: Dependency-graph tests, invalidation receipts, checkpoint reuse, and historical audit.

title: Require talking-head A-roll as the base timeline for source-led shorts
primary_story_title: Select and compile the source-backed A-roll
requirement: Every interview-derived source-led short-video Harness shall preserve the original talking-head footage and source audio as the base temporal spine; B-roll, graphics, evidence, animation, captions, and reframing may support or temporarily interrupt it only under declared functions.
required_evidence: A-roll coverage report, source-span map, support-layer manifest, and semantic review.

title: Select source spans by expression and archetype function
primary_story_title: Select and compile the source-backed A-roll
requirement: A bounded A-roll selector shall choose eligible source spans using approved Expression Moments, phrase context, speaker, audio events, keyframes, target duration, archetype sequence, and must-preserve/must-exclude constraints.
required_evidence: Source-context comparison, duration/coverage checks, candidate reasons, and independent selection review.

title: Enforce word-boundary EDL and audio continuity
primary_story_title: Select and compile the source-backed A-roll
requirement: Accepted source cuts shall snap to validated word or permitted silence/audio-event boundaries, use declared padding, preserve speaker handoffs and reactions where required, and apply production-correct audio fades and output-time caption mapping.
required_evidence: Word-boundary tests, waveform/boundary checks, caption offset validation, and rendered cut inspection.

title: Compile evidence, B-roll, captions, and motion slots as source support
primary_story_title: Compile captions, evidence, B-roll, audio, and motion support
requirement: The short-video Harness shall compile supporting visual treatments from source evidence, approved assets, keyframes, screenshots, diagrams, captions, and bounded motion-block requests, each with a declared narrative/Activative function, timing, geometry, and lineage.
required_evidence: Support-function validation, geometry checks, evidence references, and composition preview.

title: Evaluate rendered cut boundaries and whole-short coherence
primary_story_title: Evaluate rendered boundaries and repair the short locally
requirement: The evaluator shall inspect the actual rendered output at each cut, overlay transition, caption change, and selected checkpoints for visual jumps, audio pops, subtitle occlusion, wrong frames, grade consistency, source meaning, pacing, and archetype coherence.
required_evidence: Rendered timeline views, audio analysis, caption checks, VLM/human calibration, and verdict receipt.

title: Apply bounded short-video repair and operator revision
primary_story_title: Evaluate rendered boundaries and repair the short locally
requirement: Short-video failures or operator corrections shall compile into the smallest valid edit changes, preserve already-valid source selections and relationships, rerender only affected ranges or downstream outputs where possible, and stop after the configured repair limit or a human-owned boundary.
required_evidence: Change diff, invalidation map, reused checkpoint report, rerender hashes, evaluation, and human episode.

title: Produce source-grounded Carousel derivatives
primary_story_title: Produce a source-grounded Carousel
requirement: A Carousel job shall consume an approved archetype-coalition Final Script written through Guest Voice DNA, Primitive Coalition Contract, source spans, evidence, Expression Moments, and keyframes, and shall preserve source links for every slide while using Carousel-native swipe grammar.
required_evidence: Slide/source traceability, geometry/text checks, sequence evaluation, and render hashes.

title: Produce source-grounded SuperVisual derivatives
primary_story_title: Produce a source-grounded SuperVisual
requirement: A SuperVisual job shall consume an approved archetype-coalition Final Script and Primitive Coalition Contract and shall create one source-grounded, feed-legible psychological role inside tension with exact source and transformation lineage.
required_evidence: Source map, feed-size legibility, reading-order evidence, semantic evaluation, and hashes.

title: Produce source-grounded 2D animation derivatives without activating Format 02
primary_story_title: Produce a truthful non-Format-02 animation derivative
requirement: Every approved archetype-coalition Final Script shall compile a source-grounded 2D Animation Scene Package for reusable B-roll, explainer, quote, concept, and Carousel use; the active archetype template and downstream compositions decide which scenes are fully rendered, without activating Format 02.
required_evidence: Animation-job authority, source/voice map, runtime/rig receipts, identity/semantic evaluation, and Format 02 exclusion test.

title: Label quotations, condensations, voiceovers, and generated derivatives truthfully
primary_story_title: Produce a truthful non-Format-02 animation derivative
requirement: Verbatim quotations, Guest-Voice-DNA rewrites, editorial condensations, source-audio excerpts, generated voiceovers, and generated visual derivatives shall be explicitly distinguishable while preserving the approved Final Script and source lineage.
required_evidence: Quote-diff checks, voice/source metadata, operator source authorization validation, operator approval, and export disclosures.

title: Preserve cross-derivative identity, meaning, and batch continuity
primary_story_title: Evaluate continuity and export derivative lineage
requirement: The batch shall evaluate whether shorts, Carousels, SuperVisuals, and animation derivatives remain coherent in identity, terminology, evidence, visual recurrence, and intended audience movement while allowing format-native variation.
required_evidence: Cross-artifact semantic comparison, identity/source checks, recurrence analysis, and batch verdict.

title: Export complete derivative lineage and usage evidence
primary_story_title: Evaluate continuity and export derivative lineage
requirement: Every derivative output shall package source-package and span references, ingredient versions, Harness and binding identities, transformation records, generated-asset lineage, evaluations, human resolutions, publication scope, and downstream usage acknowledgements.
required_evidence: Lineage completeness test, artifact hashes, usage acknowledgement, and portable bundle validation.

title: Compile natural-language operator revisions into typed change programs
primary_story_title: Compile natural-language revision requests
requirement: A Revision Compiler Programmed Model or eligible general fallback shall translate an authorized operator request, current state, target objects, Harness, JIT context, allowed tool registry, and evaluation into the smallest typed ChangeRequestProgram or an explicit clarification/escalation.
required_evidence: Tool-call schema tests, human request benchmark, argument accuracy, over-edit rate, and interpretation receipt.

title: Convert direct manipulation into the same typed command path
primary_story_title: Commit direct manipulation through selective rerun
requirement: Dragging, trimming, splitting, resizing, cropping, reordering, selecting a candidate, or changing a parameter in the Studio shall produce the same canonical ChangeRequestProgram and validation path as an equivalent language request.
required_evidence: UI-to-command round-trip tests, undo/replay, before/after hashes, and HumanResolutionEpisode.

title: Validate revision authority, scope, and minimality before execution
primary_story_title: Compile natural-language revision requests
requirement: Every ChangeRequestProgram shall pass schema, current-state identity, authority, tool grant, Transformation Contract, source truth, geometry/timing, budget, and minimal-change validation before preview or execution.
required_evidence: Schema/authority tests, stale-state race tests, minimal-diff analysis, and admission receipt.

title: Rerun only affected work and independently evaluate the result
primary_story_title: Commit direct manipulation through selective rerun
requirement: An accepted revision shall invalidate only directly changed objects and dependent nodes, reuse unaffected checkpoints and assets, render an appropriate preview, run required deterministic and independent evaluations, and either commit, reject, or escalate.
required_evidence: Checkpoint reuse report, invalidation graph, render diffs, evaluation, and state transaction receipt.

title: Emit a HumanResolutionEpisode for every meaningful human decision
primary_story_title: Capture HumanResolutionEpisodes and use them as scoped programming material
requirement: Every approval, rejection, candidate selection, revision, manual change, tool override, escalation resolution, taste explanation, and publication decision shall emit an immutable HumanResolutionEpisode linked to the exact before/after state, operations, evidence, authority, and applicability scope.
required_evidence: Episode completeness validation, exact change/tool links, scope tests, and indexing receipt.

title: Use human-resolution evidence for governed retrieval and Programmed Model improvement
primary_story_title: Capture HumanResolutionEpisodes and use them as scoped programming material
requirement: Eligible HumanResolutionEpisodes shall become immediately retrievable precedents and may later produce supervised examples, preference pairs, hard negatives, repair trajectories, Steering Recipe evidence, or model-training candidates through offline operator-authorized dataset, shadow, evaluation, and promotion gates.
required_evidence: Indexing tests, dataset eligibility, shadow replay, claim benchmark, and promotion receipt.

title: Launch campaigns from source packages or Asset Package Specs
primary_story_title: Launch campaigns and govern autonomy and exceptions
requirement: The Studio shall let an authorized operator select a workspace/project, inspect or complete a Canonical Interview Source Package, choose target outputs and Harnesses, set seed/taste direction, budget, deadline, autonomy mode, and launch a governed content batch.
required_evidence: Order schema, source/Harness validation, budget checks, autonomy binding, and launch receipt.

title: Operate one shared Studio shell with category-native modules
primary_story_title: Launch campaigns and govern autonomy and exceptions
requirement: The product shall provide one shared workspace, project, campaign, run, review, evidence, artifact, worker, provider, budget, and publishing shell while registering Interview Expression, Video Production, Static Composition, Visual Asset, and Knowledge/Programmed Model modules behind current product boundaries.
required_evidence: Route/read-model integration tests, shared identity consistency, product-boundary tests, and accessibility review.

title: Support AUTOPILOT, REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP, CHECKPOINTED, and SHADOW modes
primary_story_title: Launch campaigns and govern autonomy and exceptions
requirement: Campaign and Harness bindings shall declare one autonomy mode, allowed human gates, publication authority, exception policy, and mode changes, with mature Harnesses targeting AUTOPILOT or REVIEW_BEFORE_SHIP rather than continuous manual editing.
required_evidence: Mode transition tests, gate/publish enforcement, exception simulations, and receipts.

title: Provide exception-only review packages and attributable decisions
primary_story_title: Launch campaigns and govern autonomy and exceptions
